steps.title: Make a table from the point and slope
steps.content: The point $(0,-7)$ is on the graph, so the $y$-intercept is $-7$. With slope $5$, moving one unit right raises $y$ by $5$, so another point is $(1,-2)$.

steps.title: Find the line from the data
steps.content: In slope-intercept form, $f(x)=mx+b$. Here $m=5$ and $b=-7$, so $$f(x)=5x-7.$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content: All four choices with $5x$ have the right slope only if their constant term is correct. The distractors use intercepts $0$, $2$, and $12$. The graph has intercept $-7$, so B is correct.
